_meta.muc_dich: Pool US day du 14 truc cho Story DNA. App random gan moi bai. AI chi ke.
_meta.ghi_chu: 5 truc moi (opening/relationship/humiliation/justice/emotion) la phan 'chat' quyet dinh da dang cam xuc va cach ke.
_meta.compatibility: Backward compatible: all original top-level pools are preserved.
_meta.engine_rule: App selects theme first, then applies theme_affinity and compatibility_rules before weighted random.
_meta.dedup_rule: Do not reuse the same story_signature inside the configured cooldown window.

villain_type: a son who runs a well-known restaurant and is ashamed of where he came from
estranged son who returned for money
wealthy sister who looks down on them
daughter who put them in a home to sell the house
son who forgot them after getting rich

twist: decorated war veteran
former trauma surgeon
retired judge
ex-military officer
the teacher who kept a struggling student in school when everyone else gave up
a decorated combat veteran who never told anyone what the citation said
retired firefighter who saved lives
former nurse in a MASH unit
secret philanthropist
the person who paid their tuition
former business owner who went broke helping family
retired police detective
Vietnam War hero
a state-champion athlete who gave up a scholarship to care for family
the one who built the family fortune
the anonymous donor who kept the local clinic open
the person who paid the villain's first mortgage payment
the quiet owner of the land beneath the family business
the witness who saved a child during a long-forgotten disaster
the retired public defender who once protected the villain's family
the person whose recipe built the successful restaurant
the original inventor behind the family company's best-selling product
the foster parent who raised several respected people in town
the veteran who carried the commanding officer to safety
the unknown beneficiary listed in a sealed legal document
ending: public recognition without any money changing hands
a brass plaque honoring their quiet service
the villain publicly shamed while the victim keeps their dignity
a community rallying around them
the truth revealed by an unexpected witness
a letter read aloud that changes everything
quiet reconciliation on the victim's terms
the victim walks away with their head high, refusing an apology
a stranger's kindness repaid decades later
the inheritance going to charity instead of the greedy relative
the grandchild choosing the grandparent over the money
a standing ovation from unlikely people
the villain left to face their own conscience
recognition from someone powerful the victim once helped
the victim's competence saving the day publicly
the family business survives only after the victim sets firm conditions
the victim chooses a modest independent life over returning to the family
the younger generation breaks the cycle and apologizes publicly
the truth changes the community's view, but the victim refuses revenge
the villain loses social standing rather than money
a private apology arrives, but forgiveness remains uncertain
the symbolic object is passed to someone who proved worthy
the victim creates a scholarship or community fund in someone else's name
the family gathers again under new rules set by the victim
the final victory is simply being believed
opening_scene: starts with a single cruel sentence the villain said out loud in front of everyone
starts with the narrator describing a small ordinary object that will matter later
starts at a formal event (wedding, funeral, gala, reunion) already going wrong
starts with the narrator quietly doing their humble job, unaware of what's coming
starts years in the past, then jumps to the moment of humiliation
starts with the narrator being handed something insulting (an apron, a bill, a back-row seat)
starts with a phone call that changes everything
starts with the narrator overhearing what family really thinks of them
starts at a will reading or lawyer's office
starts with the narrator packing up a house they're being forced to leave
starts mid-argument, the narrator staying calm while others shout
starts with a stranger showing unexpected kindness that contrasts with family cruelty
starts with the narrator looking at an old photograph
starts on an ordinary morning that becomes the worst or best day of their life
starts with the villain publicly taking credit for something the narrator did
starts with an empty chair at a family gathering and no one willing to explain it
starts with the narrator receiving a formal envelope addressed in unfamiliar handwriting
starts with a photograph being removed from a wall before guests arrive
starts with the narrator counting cash at a kitchen table late at night
starts with the sound of a lock changing on a front door
starts with a child asking an innocent question that exposes the adults
starts in a hospital waiting room where the wrong person is listed as family
starts with a public announcement that gives credit to the wrong person
starts with the narrator being mistaken for staff at an event
starts with an old object being placed in a donation box
relationship: older sibling and successful younger sibling who is ashamed of them
long-time employee and condescending young new boss
veteran and a civilian who mocks their service
widow/widower and late spouse's dismissive family
widowed mother and ambitious adult daughter
betrayed wife and husband living a double life
poor aunt and wealthy niece she once raised
older brother and younger brother who rewrote the family history
retired employee and founder's entitled child
stepmother and adult stepdaughter disputing the father's legacy
family caregiver and relatives who appeared only after the funeral
humiliation_type: seated at the back table / excluded from the family table
handed a servant's task in front of guests
publicly called by a demeaning nickname about their humble job
not introduced or acknowledged at an important event
told they're an embarrassment to the family's new status
asked to leave / not invited to their own family's celebration
having their lifelong sacrifice dismissed as 'nothing special'
being talked over and treated as invisible in the room
their handmade gift mocked or thrown aside
being pushed to sell their home or give up their savings
having their competence doubted in front of others
being corrected on manners/appearance by someone they raised
their old clothes or old car ridiculed at an upscale place
being thanked like hired help instead of family
having a private hardship exposed cruelly for a laugh
being given a separate entrance so guests will not see them
their seat being removed after they arrive
being asked to wear staff clothing during a family event
a gift they made being secretly replaced with an expensive one
being omitted from a family photograph
their name being left off an invitation or program
being told not to speak because they might embarrass the family
being denied access to a loved one in a hospital or care facility
having their belongings packed without permission
being presented with a bill for something they were promised as family
justice_type: a powerful stranger the narrator once helped recognizes them publicly
the narrator turns out to be the only one who can save the day (medical, crisis)
an old letter or document reveals a secret sacrifice the narrator made
someone the wrongdoer respects quietly confirms the narrator's version
the community rallies and publicly honors the narrator
the villain's own words are exposed / recorded / read back to them
the narrator's hidden accomplished past is revealed to the shocked room
someone the villain respects takes the narrator's side decisively
the truth about who really paid/built/sacrificed comes out
the narrator quietly walks away with dignity, refusing to beg or explain
a grandchild or younger person publicly chooses the narrator over the villain
a brass plaque, award, or public recognition names the narrator's quiet service
the inheritance/reward goes to charity, exposing the villain's greed
an unexpected witness stands up and tells the whole story
the narrator's competence saves the very person who mocked them
official records prove the narrator's version after years of denial
a child repeats the villain's cruel words in front of everyone
the narrator controls the only key document, recipe, patent, or property right
the person presumed powerless calmly ends the arrangement and leaves
the villain's attempt to profit triggers an audit or legal review
a forgotten photograph identifies the narrator as the person everyone came to honor
the family member least expected to speak finally tells the truth
the narrator's careful records dismantle the lie line by line
the victim declines money and asks only for a public correction
the institution that ignored the victim formally apologizes
dominant_emotion: bittersweet reconciliation on the narrator's terms
pride in quiet sacrifice finally seen
the warm ache of being recognized after years invisible
cold dignity - walking away with head high
tearful gratitude toward an unexpected ally
the quiet power of forgiveness that isn't weakness
vindication mixed with grief for what was lost
hope - a relationship changed, not by money but by truth
the deep calm of someone who never needed the villain's approval
slow-burn betrayal turning into moral clarity
controlled humiliation turning into public truth
grief sharpened by the discovery of deception
protective resolve - someone finally defends the vulnerable person
earned tenderness after years of emotional distance
town_setting: a working-class suburb outside a major city
a desert town along an interstate
a lakeside town dependent on summer tourism
a Southern town centered around one church
a military town near an Army base
a Rust Belt neighborhood with old brick houses
a ranching community several hours from the nearest city
a college town divided by wealth
a suburban development built over old family farmland

modest: lives on Social Security and a small pension
owns an aging paid-off house but has little cash
still works part-time despite retirement age
drives an older well-maintained pickup or sedan
rents a small apartment after losing the family home
wealthy: owns multiple businesses and values public image
lives in a gated suburban development
belongs to an exclusive country club
runs a successful law, medical, or real-estate practice
built a social-media image around family success

compatibility_rules.reason: Keep veteran stories anchored in service, duty, sacrifice, or post-service life.
New-bride stories need a younger protagonist and marriage-centered conflict.
The betrayal theme must be evidence-driven, not based only on suspicion.

justice_keywords: only one who can save the day

compatibility_rules.reason: Avoid turning every elderly-mother story into a medical miracle.
Most justice should come from dignity, sacrifice, competence, or community—not hidden wealth.

Tranh chi tiet 'qua lon' hiem gap trong doi that. Medal of Honor chi khoang 60 nguoi con song tren toan nuoc My; tuong 4 sao, huy chuong Olympic, nghi si deu cuc hiem. Doc gia 55+ co rat nhieu nguoi tung phuc vu quan doi hoac quen nguoi tung phuc vu, ho nhan ra ngay va mat tin vao ca bai. Dung phien ban DOI THUONG ma van nang: cuu binh co Bronze Star, trung ta ve huu, chu doanh nghiep dia phuong, hieu truong, bac si phong kham, thay giao cu. Nguyen tac: nguoi doc phai thay 'chuyen nay CO THE xay ra o thi tran cua toi'.
